steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the angle sum property
steps.content: The sum of all interior angles in any triangle equals $180^\circ$. We know $\angle N = 41^\circ$ and $\angle P = 41^\circ$, so set up an equation to find $\angle M$.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum formula and solve
steps.content:
The sum of angles in triangle $MNP$:
$$\angle M + \angle N + \angle P = 180^\circ$$
Substitute the known angles:
$$\angle M + 41^\circ + 41^\circ = 180^\circ$$
Combine like terms:
$$\angle M + 82^\circ = 180^\circ$$
Subtract $82^\circ$ from both sides:
$$\angle M = 180^\circ - 82^\circ = 98^\circ$$
The measure of $\angle M$ is 98, so the answer is 98.
